text: Lesson 31
zh: 第31课

text: A lovable eccentric
zh: 一个惹人喜爱的怪人

text: Why did the shop assistant refuse to serve Dickie?
zh: 为什么店员拒绝招待迪基？

text: True eccentrics never deliberately set out to draw attention to themselves.
zh: 真正的怪人从不故意做出引人注目的事情。

text: They disregard social conventions without being conscious that they are doing anything extraordinary.
zh: 他们无视社会习俗，却并未意识到自己的行为有何异常。

text: This invariably wins them the love and respect of others, for they add colour to the dull routine of everyday life.
zh: 这总能为他们赢得他人的爱戴与尊敬，因为他们为平淡乏味的日常生活增添了色彩。

text: Up to the time of his death, Richard Colson was one of the most notable figures in our town.
zh: 理查德·科尔森直到去世前，一直是我们镇上最有名的人物之一。

text: He was a shrewd and wealthy businessman,
zh: 他是个精明而富有的商人，

text: but most people in the town hardly knew anything about this side of his life.
zh: 但镇上大多数人对他这一面几乎一无所知。

text: He was known to us all as Dickie and his eccentricity had become legendary long before he died.
zh: 我们都叫他迪基，他的怪癖在他去世前很久就已家喻户晓。

text: Dickie disliked snobs intensely.
zh: 迪基极其厌恶势利之人。

text: Though he owned a large car, he hardly ever used it, preferring always to go on foot.
zh: 尽管他有一辆大车，却几乎从不使用，总是喜欢步行。

text: Even when it was raining heavily, he refused to carry an umbrella.
zh: 即使下大雨，他也拒绝带伞。

text: One day, he walked into an expensive shop after having been caught in a particularly heavy shower.
zh: 有一天，他在被一场大雨淋透之后，走进了一家高档商店。

text: He wanted to buy a ￡300 watch for his wife,
zh: 他想为妻子买一块300英镑的手表，

text: but he was in such a bedraggled condition that an assistant refused to serve him.
zh: 但他浑身湿漉漉、狼狈不堪，一名店员拒绝招待他。

text: Dickie left the shop without a word and returned carrying a large cloth bag.
zh: 迪基一言不发地离开了商店，回来时提着一个大布袋。

text: As it was extremely heavy, he dumped it on the counter.
zh: 袋子极重，他把它往柜台上一放。

text: The assistant asked him to leave, but Dickie paid no attention to him and requested to see the manager.
zh: 店员让他离开，可迪基理都不理，只是要求见经理。

text: Recognizing who the customer was,
zh: 经理一认出这位顾客是谁，

text: the manager was most apologetic and reprimanded the assistant severely.
zh: 便连声道歉，并狠狠训斥了那名店员。

text: When Dickie was given the watch, he presented the assistant with the cloth bag.
zh: 迪基拿到手表后，把那个布袋交给了那名店员。

text: It contained ￡300 in pennies.
zh: 袋子里装着300英镑的便士。

text: He insisted on the assistant's counting the money before he left--30, 000 pennies in all!
zh: 他坚持要店员当面数清这些钱才肯离开——一共三万个便士！

text: On another occasion, he invited a number of important critics to see his private collection of modern paintings.
zh: 还有一次，他邀请了几位重要的评论家来欣赏他私人收藏的现代画作。

text: This exhibition received a great deal of attention in the press,
zh: 这次展览在媒体上引起了极大关注，

text: for though the pictures were supposed to be the work of famous artists,
zh: 因为尽管这些画据说出自名家之手，

text: they had in fact been painted by Dickie.
zh: 但实际上都是迪基自己画的。

text: It took him four years to stage this elaborate joke simply to prove that critics do not always know what they are talking about.
zh: 他花了四年时间策划这场精心设计的玩笑，只是为了证明评论家们并非总是懂行。
